Tensor parity (7a) passed 8/8 with max deltas 3.3e-6 (log-mel) to 4.2e-5 (decoder logits) against atol 0.01. Metal bonus (7b): jfk RTF 0.2434 (slower than CPU, consistent with base being memory-bound) and Metal transcript verified token-for-token identical to CPU on jfk (26 tokens) plus the repo's metal e2e test green. ORT iter = WhisperProcessor feature extraction from in-memory PCM + generate(greedy, decoder_input_ids=[50258,50259,50359,50363], forced_decoder_ids=None, checkpoint suppress defaults active) + batch_decode. Corpus RTF = sum(per-file median walls)/sum(clip seconds). PRIMARY numbers = pass 2 (idle system, engines measured back-to-back). Run-to-run variance band: vokra jfk median RTF 0.645/0.665/0.691 across three idle sessions; pass-1 vokra corpus RTF was 0.821 with file 0000 environmentally inflated (11.85s -> 7.41s on re-run); pass-1 ORT corpus RTF 0.207 vs pass-2 0.241.

model: piper-plus native MB-iSTFT-VITS2 (voice: ayousanz/piper-plus-css10-ja-6lang css10-ja-6lang-fp16.onnx, 6-lang ja/en, 22050 Hz, + mathematically-neutral spk_proj adapter in GGUF)
summary: Evaluated Vokra native piper-plus vs the same voice .onnx under onnxruntime 1.19.2 CPU EP (Apple M1): piper-plus GitHub releases carry no voice assets and the canonical v7 zero-shot HF repo the runtime targets is private, so the public css10-ja-6lang voice was used; the only Apache-2.0 public voice (mera-multilingual) converts but is architecturally incompatible with the native runtime (dec.ups kernel mismatch). Since the native loader hard-requires the v7 spk_proj MLP absent from every public checkpoint, a provably neutral zero-output spk_proj was appended to the GGUF — exactly equivalent to the unmodified ORT graph fed speaker_embedding=zeros + mask=0 (its Where node selects the emb_lang-only branch). Identical real-G2P inputs (vokra-piper-g2p: EN 96 ids lid=1, JA 93 ids lid=0 with 38 prosody triples; deterministic scales [0,1,0]) produced sample-count-identical audio on both engines with mel-L1 0.00346 (EN) / 0.00330 (JA) and max sample delta <= 3 int16 LSB — near-bit-close parity. Speed (1 warmup + 3 measured, median): vokra synthesis-only estimate RTF 0.192 EN / 0.167 JA (full process 0.217/0.185) vs ORT inference-only 0.0231/0.0324, i.e. vokra ~8.3x (EN) / ~5.2x (JA) slower than multi-threaded ORT CPU (windows differ slightly and are documented). Whisper-base round-trip: JA CER 0.278 with character-identical transcripts from both engines; EN WER 1.0 on BOTH engines because the voice's deterministic EN output is unnaturally fast (1.27 s for 10 words) — a model property reproduced identically, not an engine divergence. vokra-cli's embedded --text route was exercised and documented as a placeholder char-tokenizer with stochastic default scales (drops JA text almost entirely) and excluded from the comparison.

issues: ayutaz/piper-plus GitHub releases contain NO voice .onnx assets (only CLI binaries) — voices live on HF; assignment step 1's release-download path does not exist.

The canonical zero-shot v7 checkpoint the runtime targets (ayousanz/piper-plus-zero-shot-multi-6lang-v7, named in tests/parity/piper_plus_v7*/manifest.txt) is NOT publicly accessible (HF API 401; local HF token stale per preflight). No public piper-plus checkpoint carries the v7 spk_proj MLP that the native loader hard-requires (crates/vokra-models/src/piper_plus/config.rs:136 Dims::derive).

Workaround applied and validated: appended a mathematically neutral spk_proj (zero MLP: output == 0 for all inputs) to the converted GGUF so native g = emb_lang[lid] exactly; ORT leg ran the UNMODIFIED onnx with speaker_embedding=zeros + mask=0, whose /Where node selects the emb_lang-only branch — same math. Empirically confirmed by 0 length delta and max sample delta <= 3 int16 LSB.

kizuna-intelligence/piper-plus-mera-multilingual (Apache-2.0, the only allowlist-clean public voice) converts but CANNOT load in the native runtime: tensor dec.ups.2.weight shape [64,32,8] vs expected [64,32,16] (different decoder upsample geometry) — architectural incompatibility, honest blocker for that voice.

License deviation recorded: the evaluated css10-ja-6lang voice is HF license tag 'other' with model-card license_name 'css10-public-domain' (follows the CSS10 dataset). This is explicit and maximally permissive but outside the strict Apache-2.0/MIT/CC-BY-4.0 allowlist; owner confirmation recommended. The single multilingual voice served BOTH the EN and JA sentences (no separate clean-license EN-native piper-plus voice exists publicly; piper-ljspeech is CC-BY-SA-4.0 = excluded).

vokra-cli run/bench --text TTS route uses the built-in placeholder char-level tokenizer (mirrors MockPhonemizer) with the voice's STOCHASTIC default scales — not the real G2P: JA text drops to near-empty (bench JA audio 0.0348 s = invalid), EN gets letter-wise pseudo-phonemes. Recorded (en-cli-placeholder-route.wav, bench-*.json) but excluded from parity/speed comparison; the sanctioned native route is the vokra-piper-g2p integration binary.

EN round-trip WER = 1.0 on BOTH engines: with deterministic scales [0,1,0] this JA-speaker voice speaks the EN sentence in ~1.27 s (~470 wpm) — unintelligible to whisper base. Identical sample counts and near-identical waveforms on both engines prove it is a voice/model property, not a runtime divergence. JA round-trip is fine (CER 0.278, transcripts character-identical across engines).

Speed windows are not perfectly like-for-like: vokra synthesis-only is a subtraction estimate (process minus --dump run) that still includes WAV write; ORT is pure session.run(). ORT ran with default multi-threading; no thread knobs were set on either engine.
EN ORT wall times showed run-to-run variance (0.0273/0.0293/0.0512 s); median used per the rule.

model: Mimi (kyutai/moshiko-pytorch-bf16 tokenizer-e351c8d8-checkpoint125.safetensors @ rev 2bfc9ae6, CC-BY 4.0) + DAC (descriptinc 24 kHz/8 kbps release 0.0.4 weights_24khz.pth, MIT)
summary: Both real checkpoints downloaded and sha256-verified against the parity-rvq-real.yml pins, converted to GGUF with the prebuilt vokra-cli (mimi: 319 tensors incl. derived 32x2048x512 effective tables, 518.9 MB, 10.7 s; dac: 558 tensors with 32 weight-norm-folded quantizers, 301.1 MB, 0.5 s after dac_prepare_checkpoint.py). Full-table reference dumps were generated in a fresh venv at the exact workflow pins (torch 2.9.1 / moshi 0.2.13 / descript-audio-codec 1.0.0, seed 7, 64 frames), and the env-gated cargo test real_codec_parity harness passed 2/2 with all 106 per-tensor rows PASS at atol=0.01: Mimi tables max 5.722e-6 and decode 1.526e-5; DAC codebooks and biases bit-exact (0.0), weight-norm fold max 1.192e-7, decode 4.768e-6. MAJOR finding: that raw-512 interface is NOT official silero v5 usage — the official python wrapper prepends a 64-sample rolling context ([1,576]), and without it BOTH engines' probabilities collapse on real speech (vokra max prob 0.0365 across all 3 clips, 0 speech segments at the official threshold 0.5), while ORT in official ctx576 mode yields normal probs (max 0.9999) and 4/1/1 segments — vokra-vs-official IoU = 0.0 on every file, mean|Δ| 0.69-0.86; the repo's synthetic parity fixture (reference max prob 0.0020) could not surface this, so vokra's shipped VAD is numerically faithful but functionally unable to detect real speech. Speed (median of 3 after 1 warmup, load excluded): vokra VAD RTF 0.019-0.021 (single-thread) vs ORT 0.0038-0.0040 (intra_op=1, raw512) — ORT ~5x faster; CAM++ per-embedding wav→emb vokra 0.40/0.41/0.71 s vs ORT 0.070/0.044/0.066 s (default threads) / 0.178/0.096/0.161 s (1 thread), thread models differ and sibling agents added load noise (documented). summary: Converted the pinned Kokoro-82M checkpoint via the CI recipe (download-kokoro.sh → kokoro_prepare_checkpoint.py → vokra-cli convert --model kokoro) and the parity acid test passed 9/9 tensors (prosody_f0 2.619e-2 within its documented 0.05 architectural bound, all others within 0.01, durations bit-exact). No text-level or CLI kokoro path exists (TtsEngine::synthesize is NotImplemented; vokra-cli run lacks the arch), so e2e ran at PHONEME-ID level through a scratch harness calling the public synthesize_phonemes API. Feeding byte-identical ids/style/speed to the onnx-community v1.0 export (initializers verified byte-identical to the .pth) exposed that the export plus a true-upstream kokoro-0.9.4 code oracle agree with each other to <=1.7e-5 (pred_dur exactly 57), while vokra and its own reference dumper — mutually consistent — produce 902 frames with bert Δ5.84 and text_encoder Δ1.62 vs upstream truth, i.e. the 9/9 parity proves self-consistency of a same-authored re-implementation pair, not upstream fidelity. Root causes pinned in source: LeakyReLU 0.1 vs upstream 0.2, shared ALBERT layer applied 4x vs config's num_hidden_layers=12 (and gelu vs gelu_new), decoder fed length-regulated BERT features instead of upstream's text_encoder t_en, zero F0/N contours, and ceil-vs-round duration adapters. On a real sentence (misaki G2P, af_heart voice) vokra output is unintelligible (whisper-base transcript empty, WER 1.0; mel-L1 0.53 raw / 0.40 normalized vs ORT against a 0.02-0.11 same-pipeline noise floor) while ORT with the true 256-dim style transcribes perfectly (WER 0.0). Speed (CPU, forward-only, median of 3 after 1 warmup, concurrent sibling load): vokra RTF 3.37 sentence / 3.06 fixture vs ORT 0.79 / 1.24 (single-thread ORT 1.71) → vokra is ~4.3x slower by RTF (3.3x by wall on identical tokens) and far above real-time.

issues: No CLI/text-level kokoro e2e exists: vokra-cli run supports only whisper/silero-vad/piper-plus/csm/moshi archs, and KokoroTts as TtsEngine returns NotImplemented (G2P out of scope per ADR); e2e was executed at phoneme-id level via a scratch harness (outside the repo) calling the public synthesize_phonemes API — 'The quick brown fox...' was tokenized externally with the canonical misaki G2P.

MAJOR upstream-fidelity gap: vokra native and its own PyTorch reference dumper are mutually consistent (9/9 parity PASS) but both deviate from true upstream Kokoro-82M semantics — on identical fixture inputs, ONNX export + kokoro-0.9.4 code oracle agree to <=1.7e-5 while vokra fixtures differ by bert max|Δ|=5.84, text_encoder max|Δ|=1.62, pred_dur 902 vs 57; real-sentence output is unintelligible (whisper transcript empty, WER 1.0 vs ORT 0.0 with natural style). The committed parity fixtures validate re-implementation self-consistency, not upstream truth.

Root causes found in source: (a) text_encoder LeakyReLU slope 0.1 (crates/vokra-models/src/kokoro/nn.rs LRELU_SLOPE, carried from piper-plus) vs upstream nn.LeakyReLU(0.2) (kokoro/modules.py); (b) shared ALBERT layer applied 4x (BERT_N_LAYERS=4 in dumper + Rust) vs upstream config.json plbert.num_hidden_layers=12, plus plain gelu vs transformers AlbertConfig default gelu_new; (c) decoder consumes length-regulated BERT features while upstream feeds text_encoder t_en (asr = t_en @ aln); (d) decoder gets zero F0/N contours (documented in-code) vs upstream F0Ntrain outputs; (e) mainline durations ceil(exp(log_dur)) piper-adapter vs upstream round(sigmoid-sum).

vokra kokoro CPU speed is far above real-time on M1 (RTF ~3.0-3.5 across both paths and both inputs) and ~4.3x slower than onnxruntime CPU by RTF (3.3x by wall on identical tokens); measured under concurrent sibling-agent load (run variance e.g. ORT 0.51-0.88 RTF), medians reported.

The parity fixture token entry is a seed-derived random 24-id sequence with a random unit-norm 128-style (not a phrase): fixture-leg audio is non-speech by construction (vokra peak |x| ~106 required peak-normalization for WAV writing, factors recorded; no reference text exists so fixture WER is not meaningful — transcripts reported verbatim).

model: CosyVoice2-0.5B (FunAudioLLM/CosyVoice2-0.5B @ HF revision eec1ae6c79877dbd9379285cf8789c9e0879293d, Apache-2.0; llm.pt sha256 b144ef55b51ce8cfb79a73c90dbba0bdaba4e451c0ebcfab20f769264f84a608, 2,023,316,821 B, 295 tensors all-float32; backbone = Qwen2-0.5B: 24 layers / hidden 896 / 14 q-heads / 2 kv-heads / ffn 4864 / vocab 151936 / rope_theta 1e6 / rms_norm_eps 1e-6)
summary: Downloaded the real CosyVoice2-0.5B weights and executed the M3-09 flip-the-switch: in-tree the harness is still a stub (both cosyvoice2::llm::parity::assert_vs_hf_reference and LlmWeights::from_gguf are unconditional NotImplemented; the only env gate VOKRA_COSYVOICE2_GGUF drives a load-only smoke with no tensor assertions), so the real-weight binding was performed out-of-tree through the public LlmBackbone/LlmWeights API in a scratch crate under out/ with the repo untouched. Implementation parity is tight: vokra's forward on the real Qwen2 backbone matches a bias-less PyTorch f32 mirror of exactly the ops it models to max|delta| 1.45e-4 at 24 layers (per-depth 1.4e-5 to 1.45e-4, all within the documented atol 0.01; argmax 10/10). Real-checkpoint parity vs the TRUE model FAILS architecturally: the deployed checkpoint carries q/k/v attention biases (layer-0 max |k_bias| 62.5) that LlmBlockWeights has no fields for, giving max|delta| 12.92 / mean 0.856 / argmax 0/10 against the transformers-4.57.6 eager reference (which my with-bias mirror reproduces to 2.1e-5, validating the reference) — a raw FAIL, reported untuned. Conversion works end-to-end (llm.pt -> f32 safetensors -> vokra-convert --model cosyvoice2 -> 2.57 GB GGUF verify-loads; gated in-repo smoke passes with the env set), but the converter still writes 0-placeholder arch hparams so the runtime binds llm=None (T04 residue). e2e text-to-audio is not wired for real weights at any stage — synthesize()=NotImplemented (T06 tokenizer, T07/T08 real binder, T10/T11 CFM estimator net, T13 Mimi codebooks all missing), vokra-cli run rejects the arch with exit 1, and only the injected-closure pipeline with an identity Mimi decoder runs — so no audio synthesis was attempted (ran=false for the e2e part). Additional upstream finding: the release contains no Mimi codec at all (it ships the FSQ speech_tokenizer_v2 with speech_token_size 6561, a mel-producing flow, and the HiFT vocoder hift.pt), so the module's MimiBridge design has no corresponding tensors in the actual CosyVoice2-0.5B weight set and flow.pt/hift.pt have no vokra-side consumer.

issues: In-tree flip-the-switch harness does not exist yet: cosyvoice2::llm::parity::assert_vs_hf_reference(&config, &reference_logits) and LlmWeights::from_gguf are both unconditional VokraError::NotImplemented stubs (T02/T07 residue); there is NO reference-dump file format and NO reference env var — the only env gate is VOKRA_COSYVOICE2_GGUF whose gated body (parity_cosyvoice2_gguf_smoke) asserts only GGUF-load + sample_rate==24000, so its PASS is smoke-level, not tensor parity. Real-weight parity in this eval was executed out-of-tree via the public LlmBackbone/LlmWeights API (scratch crate under out/, repo untouched).

ARCHITECTURAL BLOCKER measured on the real checkpoint: the deployed Qwen2 backbone has q/k/v projection biases per layer (layer-0 max|q_bias|=51.13, |k_bias|=62.49) which LlmBlockWeights cannot represent; bias-less vokra forward vs the true model = max|delta| 12.92, argmax 0/10 (FAIL at atol 0.01). LlmBlockWeights needs bias fields (or bias folding) before the T02 real-weight binder can ever pass — vokra's math is otherwise correct (1.45e-4 vs bias-less mirror at 24 layers).

vokra-convert --model cosyvoice2 writes 0-placeholder arch hparams (vocab/hidden/n_layer/n_head/ffn all 0) even though every value is derivable from checkpoint tensor shapes (T04 residue); consequence: CosyVoice2Tts binds llm=None on a real converted GGUF.

Upstream design mismatch: the CosyVoice2-0.5B release contains NO Mimi codec — it ships speech_tokenizer_v2.onnx (FSQ, speech_token_size 6561 per cosyvoice2.yaml), a mel-producing flow (flow.pt) and the HiFT vocoder (hift.pt); the vokra MimiBridge (8x2048x512 Kyutai defaults) has no corresponding upstream tensors, and flow.pt/hift.pt have no vokra-side consumer. The unmodeled llm_decoder [6564,896]+bias is the actual speech-token head used in generation (vokra models only the tied text head).

e2e TTS ran=false: CosyVoice2Tts::synthesize is unconditionally NotImplemented (missing T06 tokenizer, T07/T08 real LLM binding, T10/T11 CFM estimator network, T13 Mimi codebook binding, T14/T15 pipeline promotion); vokra-cli run exits 1 with 'unsupported model arch `cosyvoice2`' (engine does not route the arch; bench-only). Expected M3-09 owner residue — no audio was fabricated.

Upstream ships no monolithic safetensors (llm.pt/flow.pt/hift.pt are torch pickles; CosyVoice-BlankEN/model.safetensors is the Qwen2 base init, not the deployed weights), so the converter's documented safetensors input had to be produced by exporting llm.pt to safetensors (f32, verbatim names) in the venv.

summary: Whisper medium (real openai/whisper-medium fp32 safetensors, 3.06 GB) converted cleanly to Vokra GGUF (947 tensors, 7.9 s) via the parity-whisper-real.yml recipe and evaluated end-to-end on CPU against an optimum/onnxruntime-1.19.2 baseline exported from the same checkpoint; per assignment 7a the corpus was truncated to jfk + the first 4 LibriSpeech manifest utterances (48.25 s audio) and Metal/tensor-parity legs were skipped. Accuracy is identical between engines — aggregate WER 1.75% / CER 0.82% (jiwer + EnglishTextNormalizer on hyp+ref) and 0 cross-engine normalized word diffs over 114 words; the only 2 reference mismatches (Layton vs LEIGHTON, an vs IN) are shared by both engines, and both jfk transcripts are byte-identical and correct. Decode configs were verified like-for-like: vokra's GGUF bakes decoder_start_ids [50258,50259,50359,50363] (en/transcribe/notimestamps) and an ORT id-probe confirmed the same effective prefix with no timestamp tokens (difference: ORT applies the checkpoint suppress lists, vokra applies none — no word-level effect here). Speed strongly favors ORT: inference-only median RTF (model preloaded, 1 warmup + 3 iters) is vokra 4.26 jfk / 6.03 corpus vs ORT 0.58 jfk / 0.77 corpus, i.e. vokra ~7.4-7.8x slower (ratio_ort_over_vokra 0.135/0.127), with one-time ONNX export costing 50.3 s (+10.1 s save) and loads of ~4.5-18 s (vokra, derivation confounded by host-load variance — both raw sets reported) vs 6.3 s (ORT). vokra bench jitter was high on 2/5 files (up to 11.6 s spread from concurrent sibling agents), so medians are reported with raw stats archived. All raw logs, transcripts, per-file TSVs and scripts live under /Users/inamotoyuuta/.cache/vokra-eval/out/asr-whisper-medium/; no tracked repo file was modified.

model: mistralai/Voxtral-Mini-3B-2507 (Apache-2.0, not gated; 762 tensors / 9.356 GB bf16 = ~4.68B params; precast to F16 for conversion)
summary: Voxtral Mini 3B real-weights eval on M1/16GB (gates passed: hw.memsize 17179869184, 199Gi free; 9.36GB shards downloaded in 207s ≈ 45 MB/s): the standing M3-10 owner deliverable — real-safetensors inspection → adapter.json — is delivered and empirically verified: AudioAdapter::from_gguf binds the real multi_modal_projector tensors (active=true, Mlp [5120→3072→3072] gelu, shapes confirmed against shard headers), and a tekken.json-derived compact-vocab tokenizer blob decodes through the repo runtime (decode([1,22177,2]) → "Hello"). Conversion to GGUF succeeded (F16 verbatim, 762/762 real tensors, full vokra.voxtral.* hparams from config.json/params.json + tokenizer + adapter chunk, 9.354GB in 209s) via the public library API convert_voxtral_file_with_adapter_config, after a measured bf16→f16 precast (overflow_to_inf=0, flush_to_zero=9895 of ~4.68B, max|w|=8.8125); the stock CLI also exits 0 but writes sentinel hparams and no tokenizer (--config is unused for voxtral, a marked T04 follow-up), and on raw bf16 input it produces a weightless 1,696-byte GGUF with exit 0 ("0 float weights written, 152 non-float skipped" — BF16 missing from is_float_dtype). Inference is architecturally blocked, so ran=false and no jfk transcript or RTF exists (nothing fabricated): TextDecoder::load rejects the real GQA geometry — verbatim "voxtral text_decoder: `language_model.model.layers.0.self_attn.q_proj.weight` shape [4096, 3072] != expected [3072, 3072]" (n_heads 32 × head_dim 128 = 4096 ≠ hidden 3072; loader assumes attn width == hidden). Even after that, three more gaps block real ASR: the checkpoint's lm_head is UNTIED from embed_tokens (byte-compared unequal; vokra assumes tied logits), the audio-encoder transformer stack is a documented T19+ stub (conv-stem only), and the projector needs ×4 frame-stacking (params.json downsample_factor=4) which no vokra adapter kind can express (downsample_linear avg-pools; apply() receives hidden 1280 vs in_dim 5120). Additional findings: the converter's mini shape table (3072, 28 layers) mismatches the real 30-layer checkpoint and silently falls back to name "voxtral-unknown" via unwrap_or (contradicting derive_name's FR-EX-08 docstring), and vokra-cli run does not route the voxtral arch at all (inference attempted via a scratch harness path-depending on the repo crates; repo untouched).

issues: BLOCKER (runtime): TextDecoder::load (crates/vokra-models/src/voxtral/text_decoder.rs:141,144) requires q_proj/o_proj [hidden,hidden] and derives head_dim = hidden/n_head_q; real Voxtral-Mini-3B-2507 has num_attention_heads=32 x head_dim=128 = 4096 != hidden 3072 -> verbatim error: "voxtral text_decoder: `language_model.model.layers.0.self_attn.q_proj.weight` shape [4096, 3072] != expected [3072, 3072]". Real weights cannot load under any honest hparam choice; jfk transcription impossible (ran=false).

BLOCKER (converter, bf16): models/voxtral.rs is_float_dtype (lines ~837-842) omits GgmlType::BF16 (which vokra-core safetensors.rs:289 maps and moshi.rs decodes) -> converting the raw bf16 shard yields exit 0 and a 1,696-byte GGUF: "0 float weights written, 152 non-float skipped" (weightless success-shaped output; empirical probe saved). Workaround used: offline bf16->f16 precast (measured lossless for this checkpoint: 0 overflow-to-inf, 9895/4.68e9 flush-to-zero).

FINDING (converter shape table): derive_name expects voxtral-mini-3b = (d_text=3072, n_layer=28) but the real checkpoint has 30 layers; convert() wraps the error in .unwrap_or("voxtral-unknown") so the mismatch is silent (contradicts derive_name's own FR-EX-08 'explicit error' docstring). Also the mode field is dual-use: derive_name reads "mini"/"small" from the same VoxtralConfig.mode that becomes vokra.voxtral.mode (runtime accepts only "asr"/"s2s"), so forcing the name via mode would break the runtime.

FINDING (untied head): language_model.lm_head.weight exists and is byte-UNEQUAL to embed_tokens.weight (torch.equal=False, both [131072,3072]); vokra assumes tied logits (converter doc + TextDecoder token_emb-as-head) -> wrong logits matrix even after the GQA fix.

FINDING (encoder stub): audio_encoder::forward runs only conv1/conv2 + pos emb; the 32-layer pre-norm transformer stack + final LN is explicitly deferred to T19+ (audio_encoder.rs NOTE) -> encoder output is not real Voxtral encoder output even when weights bind.

FINDING (adapter semantic gap): real projector consumes x4 frame-STACKED encoder frames (params.json multimodal.downsample_args.downsample_factor=4; [1500,1280] -> [375,5120] reshape) then linear_1 [3072,5120] -> GELU -> linear_2 [3072,3072]. No vokra AdapterKind expresses this: mlp cannot reshape (apply() receives hidden 1280 vs in_dim 5120 -> InvalidArgument at transcribe), downsample_linear avg-pools (not concat) and is single-linear. A frame-stack downsample kind (or encoder-side reshape) is needed for real conditioning.

ADAPTER.JSON RATIONALE (deliverable, verified): kind=mlp, tensor_prefix="multi_modal_projector.", mlp_layer_names=["linear_1","linear_2"] (weight_name defaults to "weight" -> binds multi_modal_projector.linear_1.weight [3072,5120] and linear_2.weight [3072,3072], both confirmed in real shard headers), in_dim=5120, out_dim=3072, mlp_hidden_dims=[3072] (stage widths (5120,3072),(3072,3072) match [out,in] checks), activation=gelu (config.json projector_hidden_act, applied between stages only = matches HF linear_1->act->linear_2), has_bias=false/has_layernorm=false (no such tensors in weight map). Empirically loads: AudioAdapter::from_gguf -> active=true, kind=Mlp{...} in 0.56s.

CLI gaps (in-repo comments confirm both as follow-ups): vokra-cli convert voxtral arm ignores --config and passes VoxtralConfig::default() (sentinel hparams, no tokenizer -> runtime-rejected GGUF; T04 follow-up), and vokra-cli run does not route the voxtral arch (run.rs says to drive voxtral::VoxtralAsr directly). Eval used a scratch harness crate path-depending on the repo (repo unmodified; git status clean).
Quantization: --quantize q6_k is whisper-only (CLI rejects for voxtral), so the assignment's Q6_K preference was not available; GGUF is F16 verbatim passthrough (stated honestly).

Tokenizer: repo tokenizer parses only a compact-vocab dump (SentencePiece proto reader is a follow-up; tekken/tiktoken JSON unsupported) -> built the 131072-entry compact blob from tekken.json (ids 0-999 specials, id = rank+1000; <s>=1 </s>=2 match MISTRAL_BOS_ID/EOS_ID); runtime decode verified.

Minor: vokra VoxtralConfig docstring says Mistral rope_base ~1e6; real Voxtral Mini ships rope_theta=1e8 (written from config.json). config.json max_position_embeddings=131072 vs params.json 32768; 32768 (Mistral-native params.json) was written to the GGUF and documented.
